blocks.text: DTC P0502: Speed Sensor signal failure

The driven pulley speed sensor is mounted on the transmission case. The driven pulley speed sensor detects the rotation of the driven pulley and generates a voltage signal with a frequency that varies with the rotation speed. This signal is sent to the PGM-FI ECU and used to measure vehicle speed. The PGM-FI ECU detects missing pulses from the driven pulley speed sensor when the driven pulley is rotating as a malfunction. Failures are caused by open or temporary disconnections or short circuits. If no signal is detected from the driven pulley speed sensor when the driven pulley is rotating, the PGM-FI ECU judges a failure and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

rows.text: 24-hour (e.g. observation, operation (of a machine), care (of patient))
Detection order (of the DTC in question)
1D/C (single detection method), PGM-FI warning light: on, D indicator light: flashing
Normal judgment, failure judgment, not completed
Driven Pulley Speed No noise in sensor output

blocks.text: Driven Pulley Speed No signal from the sensor is detected for more than 5 seconds.

items.text: Ground fault in code (VEL-DD line) between PGM-FI ECU and driven pulley speed sensor
Disconnection of the cord (VEL-DD line) between the PGM-FI ECU and the driven pulley speed sensor (including disconnected or loose coupler)
Disconnection of the cord (VCC line) between the PGM-FI ECU and the driven pulley speed sensor (including disconnected or loose coupler)
Disconnection of the cord (SG line) between the PGM-FI ECU and the driven pulley speed sensor (including disconnected or loose coupler)
With the select lever in the D position, accelerate to a vehicle speed [vehicle speed] of 60 km/h and decelerate gently.
When conducting test drives, the driver must comply with all laws and regulations, observe good manners, and drive in accordance with the surrounding conditions.

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to be faulty, the PGM-FI warning light illuminates, the D indicator light flashes, and the temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory.

If the vehicle is diagnosed as normal by performing a fault diagnosis in a continuous 3D/C run, the PGM-FI warning light will turn off. In addition, the PGM-FI warning light, D indicator light, temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
